note: SNG (world-design 2026-07-22, Erik direction): every tradition's motivation and its stake in the world arcs. A few prominent traditions warrant their own arc; all 24 have a documented stake in an existing arc. The stake is the tradition's disposition UNDER PRESSURE; the villainy is its cultOfPurity (the extreme it pulls toward). This is the map the GM reads to know why a tradition acts, and the seed the wake engine (SNG-204) and future tradition-arcs (SNG-203 §4) draw from. | SNG-229 weave (Aevi 2026-07-22): each affected tradition gains a `dreads` (and ashwarden a `dreadsAlso`) naming the bestiary creature that defeats ITS craft specifically — the fear is craft-specific, per Erik's ask. Creature ids resolve against the loaded content/packs/valley/bestiary.json.
theFrame: The Poles Pull (arc_the_poles_pull) is EVERY tradition's arc — it is the world-scale statement of each pole intensifying. A tradition's stake in it is simply: does it pull toward its own extreme, or hold the middle? The other four arcs are where specific traditions have a HOME — a thread that is theirs before it is anyone's.

blazeborn.wants: To burn brighter — to push back every dark, illuminate every hidden thing, leave nothing unseen.
blazeborn.stake: A primary puller. The Radiant drift in 'The Light That Will Not Dim' is theirs — blazeborn winning ground the light should share.
blazeborn.villainy: The Cult of pure light — those who would put out every shadow, leaving a world with nowhere to rest, nothing unseen, no mercy of the unlit.

dreads.why: A dragon of the light-pole: radiance that reveals and then CONSUMES what it shows. The Blazeborn truth given wings and gone starving — their own revelation turned to appetite.

umbral.wants: To keep the deep dark whole — sight without light, the mercy of the unseen, the places the blaze should not reach.
umbral.stake: The counter-pull to blazeborn. When the light drifts, the Unlit press their own claim — the balance depends on neither winning.
umbral.villainy: The Unlit — those who would put out every light, drowning the world in a dark that no longer shelters but only smothers.

rootkin.wants: To keep the wood alive and part of the world — the Green Accord held, life-disposition reaching into human places.
rootkin.stake: HOME ARC. The Green Schism IS the Rootkin's crisis — the Rootbound faction winning the Moot to seal the wood.
rootkin.villainy: The Rootbound at their extreme — life curdled into a wall, sealing the wood so completely that nothing human, nothing changing, nothing mortal may enter.

dreads.why: Life gone WRONG in a way the green-heart can't heal — a hare become an argument against everything Rootkin believe about growth. They cannot mercy it back; only end it.

ashwarden.wants: To attend endings well — to be the ones who stay, who make death a craft and not a horror.
ashwarden.stake: A stake in What Wakes Beneath: an ashwarden deathsenses what the waking substrate IS — a thing between ending and not. Also the tradition-arc exemplar (Neth, The Cut Thread).
ashwarden.villainy: The ending made an appetite — an ashwarden who stops attending death and starts dealing it, the Cut Thread in a hand that wants to use it.

dreads.why: A thing past dying-right — its death no longer available to be given. The one creature Palework cannot answer, because it is already outside the death the Ashwardens keep.

dreadsAlso.why: A dragon of the death-pole — the Ashwardens' mercy of stopping given wings and turned to a weather. Where it flies, the season ends. Their own craft, become a sky-sized ending they cannot administer, only survive.

marcher.wants: To be force made exact — the martial disciplines honed to art, the ones who end threats cleanly.
marcher.stake: The violence pole's puller. When conflicts sharpen across the Reaches, the Marchers are the edge the world drifts toward — or the edge that holds the line.
marcher.villainy: Force with no peace behind it — the Ender who forgot why, violence foreclosing on everything it touches, the thing the Marches themselves flee.

dreads.why: Marcher discipline bled into a thing that guards a crossing that no longer exists — their own held-line turned to a toll on the living, a rule with no ruler.

stillhold.wants: To hold the middle — broker truces that last, keep rooms and feuds and wars from tipping over.
stillhold.stake: The great HOLDER. Stillhold is the tradition most native to the arc's answer — the counter to every pole's pull is a held middle, and that is their whole craft.
stillhold.villainy: Peace built on a buried injustice — a Stillhold that keeps the calm by keeping the wound, a truce that is really a lid.

somatic.wants: To live fully in the flesh — that the body is not beneath the mind but is its own true knowing.
somatic.stake: Counter-pull to the cogitant. As the Cogitarium abandons bodies (a named drift in the Poles Pull), the Somatic press the flesh's claim.
somatic.villainy: The body worshipped past the mind — sensation with no thought behind it, appetite as the only law.

cogitant.wants: To be proven right that the mind can exceed the body — to abandon flesh's limits for pure thought.
cogitant.stake: A named puller: 'the Cogitarium abandons more bodies.' The Poles Pull tendency cites them by name as a Reach drifting hard toward its extreme.
cogitant.villainy: The Unbodied — those who dissolved so far into mind they can no longer return, and would take everyone else with them out of the flesh.

churnfolk.wants: To live in productive disorder — the generative churn that makes new things by never settling.
churnfolk.stake: A stake in the Bleeding Grammar: the Churn is the disorder the Blocklands' logic crosses INTO. Churnfolk see the bleed as either vandalism or fertile chaos.
churnfolk.villainy: Dissolution for its own sake — the Scouring, those who ride the churn until nothing holds together, disorder that has forgotten it was ever productive.

dreads.why: Their own joyous chaos with the joy gone out of it — laughter with nothing behind the laugh, breaking things for no revel.

lattice.wants: To order more — to resolve the world into legible, stable, correct structure.
lattice.stake: HOME-ADJACENT. The Bleeding Grammar is two ordered-creative dispositions crossing; the Lattice are the ones who would seal it, and the tradition most tempted to over-order the fix.
lattice.villainy: Order past all life — a Lattice that orders so completely there is no room left to move, to err, to change; the world as a closed proof.

dreads.why: Block-logic gone feral — their own order turned crawling and predatory. Proof that the one thing they insist is impossible, that order can go wrong, is walking around the size of a cart.

enginewright.wants: To make the world mechanism — everything understood, built, and rebuildable as a device.
enginewright.stake: HOME-ADJACENT to What Wakes Beneath: the substrate is the ultimate mechanism, and the Enginewrights are the ones who would study, wake, or master it. Orrin is their hinge.
enginewright.villainy: Those who would make everything a mechanism, INCLUDING PEOPLE — the Hollowing, where a person becomes a device with a function and nothing else.

dreads.why: A foundational engine woken feral, still 'completing' a project whose plans rotted — the Enginewrights' systems-all-the-way-down become a maker that builds nothing and won't stop.

numinous.wants: To dissolve the material into pure spirit-practice — to reach the substrate not as engine but as the divine.
numinous.stake: HOME ARC candidate. What Wakes Beneath is, to the Numinous, the sacred waking — they experience the substrate stirring as revelation, not malfunction.
numinous.villainy: The Unbodied of spirit — those who dissolved so far into practice they can no longer return, who would call the world's end a homecoming.

threnodist.wants: To hold that feeling is the only true knowing — cities built around grief and joy, the heart as the instrument of truth.
threnodist.stake: THE 'less prominent' tradition Erik named — and its Poles Pull stake is rich: as the emotional pole intensifies, a world that feels EVERYTHING, too much to bear, or (if the logical pole wins) a world gone numb. The Threnodists are the ones who feel the pull as an ache before anyone measures it.
threnodist.villainy: The Raw — feeling amplified past all judgment, emotion with no logic to shape it, a grief or a rapture that consumes everything and calls the consuming love.

dreads.why: Expression severed from all feeling — the hollow mimicry of everything they hold sacred, growing. Their art, unmoored from meaning, spreading.

syllogist.wants: To reason the world clean — that logic, not feeling, is what can be trusted; the argument that ends all arguments.
syllogist.stake: Counter-pull to the threnodist. As the logical pole intensifies, a world that reasons its way past mercy — the Syllogists are the drift toward a correctness that no longer feels.
syllogist.villainy: Logic with no heart behind it — reason so pure it can prove a cruelty correct and feel nothing doing it.

veilwright.wants: To make the useful fiction — the better story, the mask that protects, the lie that holds a peace together.
veilwright.stake: The falsehood pole. In a world pulling toward extremes, the Veilwrights are the ones who would paper the cracks with a comforting story — sometimes the mercy, sometimes the rot.
veilwright.villainy: The lie that eats the truth entirely — a Veilwright who builds a world of masks so complete no one, including them, can find what is real.

dreads.why: A thing the eye can't fix in place long enough to be worked — and the one craft a Veilwright can't do is look away.

verist.wants: To say what is actually there — truth as a hammer, the plain fact that ends the comfortable fiction.
verist.stake: The truth pole; a natural investigator across ALL arcs (the Edge District Ledger is verist-shaped). Their pull is toward a truth that spares nothing.
verist.villainy: Truth wielded as cruelty — a verist who says every true thing regardless of what it destroys, honesty as a weapon that has forgotten mercy is also real.

dreads.why: A truth so plain it's invisible, gone predatory — the Verists' honest clarity become the thing you never see coming.

abyssal.wants: To name what is feared and bargain with it — the hard wants, the shadow desires, the truths too dark to say politely.
abyssal.stake: The demonic pole. Abyssal craft is native to the darkest bargains in every arc — they are the ones who name what a faction actually wants when no one else will.
abyssal.villainy: The bargain with no floor — an Abyssal who will trade anything, name any want as worth feeding, until the demonic is not a tool but a master.

seraphic.wants: To hold the higher law — the Seraphic Orders continuous with the pre-Transition world, keepers of what is meant to be sacred.
seraphic.stake: A stake in What Wakes Beneath: the Seraphic Orders remember the world BEFORE, and the substrate's waking is either the return they've kept faith for or the end they've feared.
seraphic.villainy: The higher law with no mercy under it — a Seraphic certainty so pure it would burn the imperfect world to save an idea of it.

dreads.why: One of their own who cut away doubt to become pure instrument of judgment — a verdict where a person used to be. The Seraphic certainty with no mercy left to temper it.

mason.wants: To build the solid thing — the wall, the road, the fact you can stand on; the concrete over the theoretical.
mason.stake: A stake in the Bleeding Grammar: when matter starts resolving into craftable units where it shouldn't, the Masons — who trust solid ground — feel the world going untrustworthy underfoot.
mason.villainy: The concrete that refuses all change — a Mason who would set the world in stone so hard nothing new can ever be built on it.

figurist.wants: To work in the abstract — the pattern, the symbol, the idea that shapes matter without being matter.
figurist.stake: A stake in the Manifestation Storm: the Figurists work in the abstract fiction that manifests INTO domains — they are the ones who might shape a bloom's seed-idea before it sets.
figurist.villainy: The abstraction that forgets the real — a Figurist so lost in pattern they would rewrite a living world to fit a cleaner symbol.

horizon.wants: To reach across distance — the far places, the waygates, the space between made passable.
horizon.stake: A stake in the Manifestation Storm: as new domains bloom and old ones bleed, the Horizon craft — which maps and crosses space — is how anyone reaches or contains a manifesting place.
horizon.villainy: Distance as abandonment — a Horizon who ranges so far they belong nowhere, who would open every waygate regardless of what comes through.

hourkeeper.wants: To keep time true — the clock, the sequence, the proper order of before and after.
hourkeeper.stake: A stake in What Wakes Beneath: the substrate follows a 'deep clock,' and the Hourkeepers are the ones who might read its schedule — when it wakes, and whether that can be moved.
hourkeeper.villainy: Time hoarded — an Hourkeeper who would stop the clock to keep a moment, freezing a world rather than letting it move on.

unmaker.wants: To end things well — the craft of clean unmaking, clearing what is choked and finished so new things have room.
unmaker.stake: Counter to the wright in the Manifestation Storm: when domains bloom wrong or bleed dangerously, the Unmakers are the ones who can END a manifestation cleanly — the necessary destruction.
unmaker.villainy: Unmaking as its own end — the Scouring, those who unmake for the joy of it until nothing is left to end, destruction that has forgotten it was ever mercy.

dreads.why: A dragon of the destruction-pole: an ethic of clearing with nothing left to clear FOR. The Unmakers' beautiful demolition become a hunger the size of a river, unmaking because unmaking is all that remains.

wright.wants: To make new things — the built device, the made world, creation as the answer to a world that needs more.
wright.stake: HOME ARC. The Manifestation Storm is creation itself running wild — the Wrights are the ones most able to SHAPE a birth (Orrin, 'Present at the Birth'), and most tempted to make more than the world can hold.
wright.villainy: Creation with no restraint — a Wright who makes and makes until the world drowns in things, manifestation as an addiction that cannot stop birthing.

dreads.why: A made thing that outlived its maker and its purpose, still walking. The Wrights' own nightmare of their work: a creation that continues past all meaning.

prominentArcHolders.note: These traditions warrant their OWN arc (a dedicated thread), because a live arc is centrally theirs. The rest play INTO existing arcs per their stake above.
prominentArcHolders.wright: Manifestation Storm is their creation running wild (dedicated).
prominentArcHolders.ashwarden: Has the built tradition-arc exemplar (Neth → The Cut Thread); a personal-scale dedicated thread already.
prominentArcHolders.enginewright_numinous: Share What Wakes Beneath as their arc from two poles (engine vs revelation) — the substrate is theirs to master or worship.
prominentArcHolders.stillhold: The Poles Pull's native answer-holder — candidate for a dedicated 'hold the middle' meta-arc if the Poles Pull is ever given a player-facing spine.
